steps.title: Focus on the claim
steps.content: The student claims scat-detecting dogs produce the most precise results. In this context, precision means the smallest difference between minimum and maximum population densities. To weaken that claim, the data must show that a scat-dog study has a larger density range than at least one study using another method.

steps.title: Rule out irrelevant comparisons
steps.content: A gives one other-method range without comparing it to the Davidson scat-dog range. B compares two non-Davidson studies, so it does not test the scat-dog claim. D mentions only a maximum density, not the full minimum-to-maximum range. The relevant weakening comparison is Davidson's larger range. The answer is C.
